skills.name: ab-adr
skills.description: Architecture Decision Record — capture a technical decision after it is made: context, decision, consequences, alternatives considered, and status.

skills.name: ab-agent-eval
skills.description: Self-evaluate agent output quality — score a completed task against its acceptance criteria, surface gaps, and decide whether to re-attempt or escalate.

skills.name: ab-agent-harness
skills.description: Build the scaffolding around an agent — input/output wiring, retry envelope, logging, cost tracking, and graceful failure. The structural layer that makes an agent production-ready rather than demo-ready.

skills.name: ab-agent-introspection
skills.description: Debug a misbehaving agent — inspect its reasoning trace, tool calls, context window pressure, and output to pinpoint where it went wrong. The agent equivalent of a debugger.

skills.name: ab-agentic-engineering
skills.description: Agentic system design patterns — guide the design and review of multi-agent pipelines: tool selection, context management, retry/fallback contracts, human-in-the-loop gates, and output validation. Prevents the most common agentic failure modes.

skills.name: ab-api-design
skills.description: API design review and pattern enforcement — evaluate a proposed or existing API surface for REST/RPC conventions, naming consistency, backward compatibility, error contract, auth model, and documentation completeness.

skills.name: ab-architect
skills.description: System / component design mode. Produces a design with explicit invariants, component boundaries, data flow, and failure modes. Use before writing code for any medium+ feature or any change that touches cross-cutting concerns.

skills.name: ab-architecture-audit
skills.description: Architecture audit — review a codebase or PR for structural concerns: file size violations, coupling, missing abstractions, duplicated logic, misrouted responsibility.

skills.name: ab-article-writing
skills.description: Technical writing for docs, changelogs, postmortems, and blog posts — structured, evidence-based, audience-aware.

skills.name: ab-autonomous-loop
skills.description: Design and safety-gate autonomous agent loops — structure a recurring or self-driving agent task with explicit stop conditions, budget limits, drift detection, and human escalation triggers. Prevents runaway automation.

skills.name: ab-benchmark
skills.description: Benchmark methodology — measure baseline performance, run under load, compare before/after, surface regressions with reproducible evidence. Never benchmark without a baseline.

skills.name: ab-blueprint
skills.description: Architecture blueprint for new features or systems — produce a one-page design doc covering: problem statement, proposed solution, component breakdown, data model sketch, API surface, risks, and open questions. Decision record, not an essay.

skills.name: ab-browser-qa
skills.description: Browser-driven QA — drive a real browser (Playwright/Puppeteer) to click through the golden path, fill forms, assert UI state, and capture screenshots as evidence.

skills.name: ab-canary
skills.description: Post-deploy canary watch — monitor key metrics, error rates, and latency for a defined window after deployment. Escalate if thresholds exceeded.

skills.name: ab-code-tour
skills.description: Guided codebase walkthrough — produce a structured tour of an unfamiliar repo: entry points, data flow, key abstractions, gotchas, and a recommended reading order.

skills.name: ab-codebase-onboarding
skills.description: Rapid codebase orientation — scan structure, understand entry points, map data flows, identify conventions, and produce a terse orientation summary in chat.

skills.name: ab-debug
skills.description: Root-cause bug investigation using the scientific method. Forms a hypothesis, tests it, narrows the search, and fixes the underlying cause — not the symptom. Logs each hypothesis + result so you can't loop on the same wrong theory.

skills.name: ab-gan
skills.description: GAN loop orchestrator — Generate-Assess-Normalize triad that stress-tests output through adversarial evaluation before approving it.

skills.name: ab-graphify
skills.description: Map the entire codebase into a queryable knowledge graph stored at .platform/graphify/ — interactive visualization, a graph report, and a JSON graph file for fast cross-file research.

skills.name: ab-pm
skills.description: Product-management thinking mode. Forces user-value framing before writing code: who is the user, what problem does this solve, what's the simplest validation, what are the failure modes. Use before starting any feature or when the user asks 'should we build X?'.

skills.name: ab-qa
skills.description: Quality assurance pass — manual or real-browser testing of a feature against acceptance criteria. Produces a pass/fail report with reproducible repro steps for any failures. Use before shipping any UI-visible change.

skills.name: ab-research
skills.description: Bounded research protocol. 1 web search + 2-3 fetches + 3-5 code reads max. Produces a <=300-word synthesis in chat (never a .md file). Use before proposing a plan for any new stream or medium+ scope task.

skills.name: ab-review
skills.description: Pre-PR code review. Reviews a diff against spec compliance, code quality, security, and test coverage. Produces a structured findings list with severity. Use before every merge.

skills.name: ab-scientific-thinking
skills.description: Hypothesis-driven investigation for technical problems — form a falsifiable hypothesis, design a minimal experiment, observe results, update beliefs, repeat.

skills.name: ab-search-first
skills.description: Search-first protocol — before writing any code or answer, prove you searched existing code, docs, and memory. Surfaces reuse opportunities and prevents duplication.

skills.name: ab-security
skills.description: Security audit pass. Runs an OWASP-aligned checklist against a diff or a file set, covering auth, authorization, secrets, input validation, injection, tenant isolation, logging hygiene, and dependency risk. Produces a findings list with severity, not a 100-page report.

skills.name: ab-skill-comply
skills.description: Verify that an agent is following its assigned skill protocol — check identity header, protocol step execution, output format, and hard rule compliance.

skills.name: ab-skill-scout
skills.description: Discover, audit, and stocktake the installed skill pack — list available skills, check for stale/missing runtime copies, and suggest gaps to fill.

skills.name: ab-strategic-compact
skills.description: Strategic compact — distill a complex situation into a one-page decision brief: context, options, recommendation, risks. For competing priorities and stakeholder tradeoffs.

skills.name: ab-taste
skills.description: Code and design taste review — clarity, naming, abstraction, and craft. The review that catches what linters miss.

skills.name: ab-tdd
skills.description: Test-driven development workflow — write failing test first, implement to green, refactor, verify no regressions. Enforces red-green-refactor discipline.

skills.name: ab-team-orchestration
skills.description: Coordinate multiple specialised agents toward a shared goal — assign roles, manage inter-agent dependencies, handle partial failures, and synthesise results. The conductor skill for complex multi-agent workflows.

skills.name: ab-test-writer
skills.description: Unit test writer. Enumerates edge cases by feature type (API endpoint / business logic / auth / data mutation / UI component) and writes tests that cover happy path + boundaries + failures. Uses the project's actual test framework found in the codebase.

skills.name: ab-token-budget
skills.description: Token budget advisor — estimate task token cost, warn when approaching context limits, recommend chunking or model downgrade for cost-sensitive work.

skills.name: ab-triage
skills.description: Classify a task by type/scope/risk before starting work. Decides which workflow depth and which specialist skills are appropriate. Run this at the start of every non-trivial task.

skills.name: ab-verification-loop
skills.description: Agent verification loop — re-run verification steps until all pass or escalation threshold is reached. Prevents claiming success without evidence.

skills.name: ab-workflow
skills.description: The 6-stage inline workflow orchestrator (triage -> interview -> research -> propose -> execute -> verify). Runs a medium/large task through all stages with the right specialists at each step. Plans live in chat, never as .md files.
